Arch space for eruption of 2nd & 3rd molar created by:
\boxed{C}
**Clinical Reasoning:**
The arch space for eruption of the second and third molars is primarily established during childhood via longitudinal growth of the mandible, which includes resorption of the posterior border of the ramus and apposition of new bone on the anterior portion. This allows sufficient room for posterior teeth to erupt properly. Evidence specifically supports that the posterior border of the ramus undergoes resorption, creating space for the eruption pathways of the second and third molars.
